rationale: The orange bounding box spans approximately 228 px wide × 340 px tall (48.3 ft × 72.1 ft). The bottom ~25% of the box contains visible yard/entry pavement, so the effective roof bounding rectangle is 48.3 ft × 54 ft ≈ 2,608 sq ft. Applied an 83% fill factor for the complex, multi-section hip roof with projecting bays and turret/octagonal front elements, yielding ~2,165 sq ft, rounded to 2,200 sq ft. Ridge lines traced along the central N–S spine and secondary wing ridges (~46 ft total). Hip lines are the dominant feature with multiple sloped corner edges across all projecting sections (~112 ft). Valley lines counted at each wing-to-main-body junction, visible as darker inward angles (~50 ft). Rake edges attributed to the triangular gable/turret faces at the south front (~25 ft). Eave lines estimated along horizontal lower perimeter segments (~100 ft). Confidence is moderate due to the high roof complexity and partial occlusion of edges by roof overhangs and shadows.
